_instructions: AtlasLens landing-page text. Fill in the fields below, save, and restart the app. Leave a field as "" (empty) to hide that section on the Introduction tab. This file is read once at startup; there is no in-app editor. Keys starting with "_" are documentation and are ignored by the app. For "species", enter the NUMBER of your organism from _species_options below (e.g. "1") so GO enrichment, Ensembl-to-symbol mapping, and Hallmark gene sets use the right annotations. Leave "species" as "" to auto-detect it from the gene names. (Typing the full name still works if you prefer.)
_column_mapping: Optional. Name the metadata columns AtlasLens should use in the Time Series tab, so it does not have to guess them. Leave "" to auto-detect. 'timeseries_column' is your timepoint/day column (e.g. "Day" or "TimeSeriesInfo") - set this if the Time Series tab fails to find your timepoints. 'condition_column' splits trajectories into groups (leave "" to treat all cells of a cell type as one group). 'celltype_column' and 'dataset_column' are optional. Each value must match a column name in your object's meta.data EXACTLY (case-sensitive); an unknown name is ignored and the app falls back to auto-detection.
